name: Chinese: Stir-Fry Pork & Peppers
recipeIngredient: 1 Piece ginger root, peeled and chopped (1")
1 lb Pork tenderloin, cut into thin strips
1 Red bell pepper, seeded and cut into thin strips
1 Green bell pepper, seeded and cut into thin strips
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine Five Spice Powder, sherry, soy sauce, garlic, and ginger.
2. Add pork strips to the mixture and stir well. Let stand for 30 minutes.
3. Cut onions into eighths and layer them in separate layers.
4. Heat corn oil in a skillet or wok over medium heat.
5. Drain the pork from the marinade and reserve it.
6. Add pork to the hot oil and stir-fry until browned, about 5 minutes. Remove from the skillet and keep warm.
7. Add remaining oil to the skillet.
8. Add onions, bell peppers, mushrooms, and water chestnuts. Stir-fry for 3 minutes.
9. Add the vegetable mixture back to the pork.
10. Blend cornstarch with reserved marinade and 2 tablespoons of chicken stock in a separate bowl.
11. Add remaining stock to the skillet and bring to a boil.
12. Add the cornstarch mixture to the skillet, stirring constantly.
13. Return pork and vegetables to the skillet and heat through, stirring constantly.
14. Garnish with leek curls and green onion curls, if desired, and serve hot.
